qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution in deepHas set() via constructor.prototype path traversal
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same deepHas.js add()/indexer()-driven root cause described by the advisory: version 1.0.7 rejects only __proto__, so constructor.prototype path segments can still resolve to Object.prototype and pollute future objects.

selected_finding.title: Prototype pollution in deepHas set() via constructor.prototype path traversal

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding matches GHSA-2733-6c58-pf27 because it reaches the same constructor.prototype path traversal flaw in deepHas.js and the copied PoC rerun demonstrated end-to-end Object.prototype pollution on the vulnerable 1.0.7 code path.
evidence: The advisory summary and details name prototype pollution via constructor.prototype in deephas 1.0.7 and place the bug in deepHas.js add()/indexer() path handling.
The vulnerable source only blocks the literal substring __proto__ before add() resolves path segments and writes through initial.reduce(indexTrue, obj), which leaves constructor.prototype reachable.
The upstream fix commit 8097fafd3776c613d8066546653e0d2c7b5fc465 adds explicit rejection of __proto__, constructor, and prototype segments in deepHas.js, matching this finding's root cause.
The copied PoC run log contains EVOHUNT_PROOF: prototype pollution reached Object.prototype and JSON showing polluted=true, inherited_value=true, and object_prototype_value=true for a fresh object.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied rerun printed EVOHUNT_PROOF: prototype pollution reached Object.prototype and emitted JSON confirming a fresh object inherited evohuntAdmin=true from Object.prototype.
